rulings.Case Title: LUMINOUS ION LLC VS. VICKI CHIAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P. VS. 644 BROADWAY LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend And Supplement Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND AND SUPPLEMENT COMPLAINT is GRANTED; Plaintiff's Motion to file a Supplemental Complaint is moot. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to file the proposed Third Amended Complaint. The Court has reviewed all the supplemental allegations proposed in the Supplemental Complaint. The Third Amended Complaint should include all the new allegations, as well as the supplemental allegations contained in the Supplemental Complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate January 20, 2022 Order Sustaining Defendant Mark Smith'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint

rulings.Case Title: REINO NIEMELA ET AL VS. JEFFREY EVANS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is OFF CALENDAR, untimely. A motion to quash in an eviction case must be set for hearing within 3-7 days from filing of the motion. (CCP 1167.4(a) and CRC 3.1327(a).) If the motion to quash is served by mail than the hearing must be set between 8-12 days because of the additional 5 days required by the provisions of CCP 1013(a). (See CRC 1327(a).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LS VENTURES, LLC VS. FAUSTO YOC-MEJIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff Ls Venture, Llc'S Motion To Compel Written Discovery And Deem Admissions; Request For Sanction In The Amount Of $1,500.00
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL WRITTEN DISCOVERY AND DEEM ADMISSIONS; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500.00 is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant shall provide complete, verified responses without objections to the Requests for Production, Special Interrogatories, and Form Interrogatories (General and UD) within 5 days of notice of entry of this order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $300. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
